data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. This is because **MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time**, before targets run — so the generated file is invisible to the `Compile` item group.

The fix: explicitly add the generated file to `Compile` and register it with `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation since globs expand at evaluation time -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs are evaluated at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up by the implicit `Compile` glob.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it with `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me apply the fix using the `including-generated-files` skill knowledge.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation since globs expand at evaluation time -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause:** MSBuild implicit `Compile` globs expand at **evaluation time** (before any targets run). The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the file at **execution time**, so the glob never sees it.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to explicitly add the generated file to:
- `Compile` — so the compiler includes it
- `FileWrites` — so `dotnet clean` removes it
